report_name: Polymarket Loses $3.1M in Frontend Supply Chain Attack, Pledges Refunds

attack_patterns.name: Compromise Software Supply Chain
attack_patterns.description: Attackers compromised an unnamed third-party software dependency used by Polymarket's website to inject malicious JavaScript.

attack_patterns.name: Trusted Relationship
attack_patterns.description: Polymarket's website inherently trusted the compromised JavaScript library from its third-party vendor, allowing the malicious code to be loaded by users.

attack_patterns.name: Drive-by Compromise
attack_patterns.description: Users visiting the legitimate Polymarket website were served the malicious JavaScript from the compromised dependency, leading to code execution within their browser.

attack_patterns.name: User Execution
attack_patterns.description: The malicious script deceived users into signing what appeared to be legitimate transactions, which in fact authorized the transfer of funds from their wallets.

attack_patterns.name: Command and Scripting Interpreter: JavaScript
attack_patterns.description: Malicious JavaScript was injected into a third-party dependency to intercept user wallet interactions and present fraudulent transaction approvals.

malware.name: Malicious JavaScript
malware.description: Malicious JavaScript injected into a third-party dependency on Polymarket's website. The script intercepted user wallet interactions to present fraudulent transactions, tricking users into approving fund transfers to attacker-controlled wallets.

campaigns.name: Polymarket Frontend Supply Chain Attack
campaigns.description: A frontend supply chain attack targeting the Polymarket cryptocurrency prediction platform, where a compromised third-party dependency was used to inject malicious JavaScript and steal approximately $3.1 million from user wallets.

identities.name: Polymarket
identities.description: A cryptocurrency prediction platform that was the target of a frontend supply chain attack. The platform's website was compromised via a third-party dependency, leading to the theft of $3.1 million from its users' wallets.

infrastructure.name: Attacker-controlled Wallets
infrastructure.description: Digital currency wallets used by the attackers to receive approximately $3.1 million in pUSD stolen from Polymarket users.

infrastructure.name: Cross-chain Bridge
infrastructure.description: A cross-chain bridge was used by the attackers to launder the stolen funds by moving them from the Polygon network to Ethereum and swapping them for ETH.

indicators.name: JavaScript Loaded From Non-Allowlisted Domain
indicators.pattern:
title: JavaScript Loaded From Non-Allowlisted Domain
id: 1a2b3c4d-5e6f-4a7b-8c9d-0e1f2a3b4c5d
status: experimental
description: >-
  Detects when a web page loads a JavaScript file from a domain that is not on a pre-defined allowlist. This can be an indicator of a frontend supply chain attack, where a compromised dependency or a malicious script is loaded from an attacker-controlled domain.
references:
  - https://securityboulevard.com/2026/06/polymarket-customers-lose-3-million-in-supply-chain-attack/
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-06-28
tags:
  - attack.t1189
  - attack.t1195.002
level: medium
logsource:
  category: proxy
detection:
  selection:
    c-uri|endswith: '.js'
  filter:
    cs-host|endswith:
      # This list would need to be customized for the monitored environment
      - '.polymarket.com'
      - '.google-analytics.com'
      - '.googletagmanager.com'
      - '.jsdelivr.net'
      - '.cloudflare.com'
      - '.jquery.com'
  condition: selection and not filter
indicators.description: Detects when a web page loads a JavaScript file from a domain that is not on a pre-defined allowlist, a behavior consistent with the Polymarket supply chain attack.
